heroHeadline: Hamas-US Cairo Talks Collapse Without Progress; Ceasefire Violations Mount as Disarmament Impasse Deepens
heroSubtitle: The deadliest attack on Jewish people since the Holocaust — assault timeline, hostage crisis resolved Jan 2026, intelligence failures, and IDF response. Hamas formally rejected the Board of Peace disarmament plan on Apr 14 and walked away from Cairo talks on Apr 17 without progress. The IDF killed three Hamas commanders including a Nukhba unit participant from the Oct 7 hostage-taking. Six months into the ceasefire, Gaza residents say recovery has not begun — less than $1B of $17B pledged reconstruction funds received.
footerNote: Data sourced from IDF, Israel Police, UN OCHA, Israeli government, Reuters, AP, Haaretz, and institutional analyses. Casualty figures are official Israeli counts; Hamas-theater figures are separately noted and contested. All 251 hostages accounted for as of January 26, 2026. Cumulative conflict dead (Oct 7, 2023 – Mar 25, 2026): 72,265 Palestinians per OCHA/Gaza MoH. Ceasefire in effect since Oct 2025; 2,400+ violations documented by Gaza GMO as of Apr 17, 2026.
